title: Remove mandatory Internet Identity authentication for guestbook entries

requirements.text: Remove the authentication check in the backend that prevents anonymous users from adding guestbook entries. Any visitor should be able to submit a guestbook entry without being signed in via Internet Identity.

quotes: Mandatory signing in would be a dead end feature because almost no one has an internet computer account
anyone can sign the guestbook anonymously — no account required

acceptanceCriteria: The backend addEntry (or equivalent) function no longer checks for an authenticated caller before allowing the entry
An anonymous principal is accepted and an entry is successfully stored
No 'Unauthorized' error is returned for unauthenticated calls to add an entry

requirements.text: Update the AddEntryPage so that authentication is no longer required to submit the form. Remove any sign-in gate, error message, or disabled state that blocks submission for unauthenticated users. The form should be fully accessible and submittable without logging in.

quotes: Visitors would just enter their name and message directly
anyone can sign the guestbook anonymously — no account required

acceptanceCriteria: The submit button on AddEntryPage is enabled and functional without signing in
No sign-in prompt or authentication error is shown when a visitor tries to submit an entry
A visitor can enter their name and message and successfully submit the form anonymously

constraints: Do not remove the Internet Identity login/logout button from the header — users who have an account can still choose to log in
Do not change any other access control logic (e.g., edit/delete controls that belong to an entry's creator)
nonGoals: Removing Internet Identity integration entirely from the app
Changing the visual design or layout of any page
Adding any new fields to the guestbook entry form
